remarks.text:
A conjecture of Erd\H{o}s and S\'{a}rk\"{o}zy. Some condition relating $A$ and $B$ is necessary since, for example, if $A$ is the set of all integers with only even binary digits and $B$ is the set of all integers with only odd binary digits then $1_A\ast 1_B(n)=1$ for all $n$.

This is a stronger form of [28]. See also [331].

statement.text:
Let $A=\{1\leq a_1<a_2<\cdots\}$ and $B=\{1\leq b_1<b_2<\cdots\}$ be sets of integers with $a_n/b_n\to 1$. 

If $A+B$ contains all sufficiently large positive integers then is it true that $\limsup 1_A\ast 1_B(n)=\infty$?
